front.title: Protected Orchestrated Overlay Link (POOL): A Secure Self-Healing Transport Protocol

abstract.t: This document describes the Protected Orchestrated Overlay Link (POOL), an experimental secure transport protocol. POOL provides mandatory mutual authentication, always-on authenticated encryption with no plaintext mode, a stateless handshake resistant to resource exhaustion attacks, cryptographically unpredictable sequence numbers, self-describing 256-bit addresses, active path MTU discovery, per- flow telemetry, atomic configuration changes with automatic rollback, and an append-only hash-chained change journal. POOL operates either as an overlay above TCP or directly over IP using experimental protocol number 253.
